body_txt: Imagine if you will, Colonel McGoodguy and The Squad are in a pinch. They're all out of ammo, the Redshirt Army is knee deep in Mooks on the other side of town, their Military Mashup Machine is a smoldering pile of scrap, and even though The Smart Guy managed to Reverse Polarity and get a second shot out of the Applied Phlebotinum that only works once , it's not enough. General Shagnasty and his goons have them dead to rights, and they're staring down the barrels of way more BFGs than they should have left after our heroes blew up their factory two episodes ago. The villains gloat, because nothing can stop them now ! But something does. An unstoppable onslaught sends the villains running for cover! The day is saved! ...wait, aren't those the Elite Mooks from season two ? ... Dr. Bludengutsen ? Is that you? Why are you helping us? (Which might imply that there is an unspoken and bizarre sense of honor between us that we didn't know about until just now?) Well, there are several flavors of this trope, such as: Not to be confused with Save the Villain when a hero saves a bad guy or Bad Guys Do the Dirty Work when the bad guys do something that the good guys were too squeamish to do. May also be a case of Terrifying Rescuer until the heroes figure out what's really going on ( especially if the rescuer is some form of monster ).
